title: HYPOTHESIZED (post-MOD-1 reframe): symbolic-channel selection on codon identity opposes material-channel perturbation on the same mutations

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'HYPOTHESIZED (post-MOD-1 reframe): symbolic-channel selection on codon identity '
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Genome-wide Ti/Tv ratio of fixed substitutions on the human lineage relative to the inferred ancestral sequence (Ensembl).
Estimated transition/transversion ratio in the unselected germline mutational input (Kong et al. 2012 deCODE family trios), prior to selection.

statistics.name: titv_unselected_germline_input
statistics.note: Pre-selection germline mutational input Ti/Tv estimate from Kong 2012; reported in MOD-3 context.

statistics.name: titv_enrichment_fixed_over_input
statistics.note: Derived: s_titv_fixed / s_titv_germline_input. Selection enriches transitions ~3.4-fold over the unselected expectation.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts establish the empirical premise on which the hypothesis stands: (1) the human fixed Ti/Tv must be substantively above 1.0 (transition-biased fixation); (2) the enrichment over the unselected germline input must be substantial (>2x), establishing that selection — not mutational input — produces the bias. Note: licensing the inference licenses the empirical premise that 'fixation is transition-biased and selection-driven.' The dual-channel SELECTION mechanism is HYPOTHESIZED on top of this; the statistics here do NOT directly demonstrate symbolic-channel-overriding-material-channel at the per-mutation level. That would require a per-mutation comparison of thermodynamic perturbation (loud) vs codon impact (silent transitions in 4-fold-degenerate sites are over-represented), which is queued as a separate sibling claim that this claim depends on.
inference.failure_mode: If s_titv_fixed fell to ~ 0.6 (matching the input), the empirical anchor for the dual-channel hypothesis would dissolve — there would be no fixation bias for selection to explain. Note: even if the inference is licensed, the conclusion is registered as qualified_positive (HYPOTHESIZED), reflecting MOD-1's downgrade of the original 'demonstrates dual selection' framing.

conclusion.assertion: Human fixed substitutions show a Ti/Tv ratio of approximately 2.05, ~3.4-fold enriched over the unselected germline mutational input. This is empirically consistent with — but does not demonstrate — a dual-channel framework in which symbolic-channel selection (codon identity, biased toward synonymous transitions in 4-fold-degenerate sites) opposes material-channel perturbation (transitions disrupt stacking thermodynamics more severely than transversions on average). The hypothesis is registered as qualified_positive pending per-mutation verification.

scope.scale_note: Genome-wide Ti/Tv comparison. Per-codon and per-position decompositions live as sibling claims; the cost-expression gradient (cost_expression_gradient_human) is the partner claim that this hypothesis depends on for its symbolic-channel motivation.

confidence.note: Hypothesis register: confidence reflects the pattern of evidence, not a single-test p-value.

external_assumptions.statement: The MOD-1 reframe from 'demonstrates dual selection' to 'is consistent with' / 'hypothesized' is the correct epistemic register; the two correlations in the paper (cost-expression and Ti/Tv) jointly suggest but do not prove opposing selective regimes on the same mutations.

Kong et al. 2012 deCODE family-trio mutational input estimate is a defensible upper bound for the unselected human germline Ti/Tv; alternative estimates (1KG de novo, gnomAD low-frequency) would shift the enrichment ratio modestly but not its sign.

This claim supersedes the paper2 v1 draft framings 'reflects symbolic-channel selection' and 'demonstrates dual selection' (both downgraded by MOD-1). v1.1 schema does not yet have first-class supersedes/superseded_by fields; the supersession is documented in dual_channel/claims/README.md (claim 7) until v1.2.
